text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, Chapter Background — p.192.

Chapter 4 — A History of Ashes. The PCs ride into the Cinderlands to win the Shoanti's trust — and the lore of Kazavon. Begin at L10/11; reach 12th before the Deathhead Vault; 13th by chapter's end.

The history (GM): centuries ago the blue dragon Kazavon, champion of Zon-Kuthon, conquered Belkzen from Castle Scarwall — until Mandraivus of the Order of the Palatine Eye slew him with the bastard sword Serithtial. The dragon's remains would not be destroyed; seven survivors each carried one relic away in secret. The Shoanti shaman Amarund took the fangs — 'Midnight's Teeth' — to the Thassilonian pyramid on Conqueror's Bay… where Korvosa now stands. Her line guarded them for three hundred years. Ileosa found them anyway. And the Shoanti, watching their queen-enemy rise, now sharpen blades for a war that would only feed her.

NPC developments: Amin (if escorted out) runs with Cressida's rebels in the Gray District; Pilts (if alive) executes Cerulean Society envoys for sport — Boule may want him dead; Salvator (if rescued) leaves for Nidal with Laori's Brotherhood; if left behind, he dies trying to escape.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, Part 1: The Road North — p.194.

The PCs escort Vencarlo and Neolandus out of Korvosa to Harse. (If they sent the two ahead and stayed, Cressida urges them to follow — plenty of good folk remain to mind the city; the real solution to Ileosa lies north.) Cressida's parting kit: a wand of sending (10 charges), 2 scrolls of dream, and 4 scrolls of teleport for emergency returns (Bishop Keppira d'Bear of Pharasma relays sendings if no PC can). Returning mid-chapter to resupply or check in is fine.

Blackbird Ranch: five minutes north of Harse on the Sarwin — Jasan Adriel's spread (ex-adventurer, 'the Blackbirds,' Vencarlo's old comrade; their coded letters left the queen no paper trail to follow). Trinia Sabor is here, leveled-up and delighted. After dinner, in the basement, Neolandus gives the full Kazavon Situation briefing (the Background above, as he knows it) and Vencarlo asks for the amulet of proof against detection from the Blackjack cache, if found, to hide Neolandus. Jasan outfits the party: trail rations, light warhorses, pack animals — free.

Trinia's reward parcel: her own adventures paid off — she presses some of the 'new magic' she's discovered on the PCs (draw a Lvl-10-appropriate consumable parcel from her updated NPC kit, Appendix 1; scrolls and a wand suit her).

The Fourth Harrowing en route — chapter suit: Hammers (Strength); award and spend Harrow Points per Appendix 3. Past = Korvosa's founding on stolen ground; present = the PCs as fugitives mirroring the Shoanti exile; future = a city dangerous but topple-able. Foreshadow Cindermaw, the Moon temple, Bolt Rock, and the vault — strength matters now, but they are not yet strong enough for the queen herself.

The journey: the only easy land route up the Storval Rise is through anarchic Kaer Maga — no chapter events there, but it is the region's best place to sell loot, buy gear, and stage (Korvosa being under martial law). Beyond the Rise, run Cinderlands travel by the Appendix 2 environment rules and the regional random-encounter table.

Factions in play all chapter: the Brotherhood of Bones (Shadowcount Sial + a chained kyton join @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori} in Kaer Maga; they scry the PCs and won't approach until Scarwall — give Laori's chosen target occasional chances to notice the scrying; if the party frets over it, Laori spends a wand-of-sending charge to apologize for spying, 'just curious!' — early contact at your discretion); the Red Mantis — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} runs the hunt from Korvosa via @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu's} sendings, hiring @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderlander0001]{the Cinderlander} as guide and buying the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ashwingGargoyl01]{Ashwing gargoyles} with Mediogalti gold. Two scripted ambushes in Part 2; the reckoning in Part 3.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, Part 2: Trials of Respect — p.200.

Respect Points (RP) — the chapter's spine, run as PF2e Victory Points. 20 RP → Thousand Bones declares the PCs worthy and the Sun Shaman speaks. −10 RP → branded enemies of the people; only battle remains. Show the running total; never reveal the target. Failing a quest costs nothing.

  • Shoanti PCs: +1 each (once); an exposed impostor (e.g. illusory disguise): −3.
  • Trophies: the severed head of a CR-9+ enemy of the Shoanti (great orcs, giants, dragons, the Cinderlander…), presented publicly to an elder within a week: +1 each, max 10 from trophies (@Check[type:society|dc:15] or any Shoanti to learn the custom).
  • Quests: per their entries (sredna +1–3; Akram +3; Cindermaw +2/+4; the Mark +3; the Trial +1/+1 per totem; Flameford +4; saving Krojun +3; Diplomacy at the Fire +2).
  • Losses: dead boneslayers −1d4 each; thundercallers routed and bad-mouthing −1d6; talking the moon maidens out of their fight −2d4; Krojun-rescues-the-party −1d6; refusing sredna −1; killing Cindermaw −1; public disrespect at GM discretion. The total can go negative.
  • Order matters: approaching the Lyrune-Quah or Sklar-Quah before a peaceful Skoan-Quah contact raises every difficulty sharply — the Sun Clan attacks intruders; the Moon Clan simply melts away.

The hostile-path fallback (−10 RP, or open war): the campaign must still reach Scarwall. If the party is branded enemy and the Gaekhen goodwill is squandered, put an ancient stone tablet carrying the needed lore — Mandraivus, Serithtial, and Castle Scarwall — on the Sun Shaman's person as a battle prize. Won in blood, the road east still opens; it merely opens friendless (no Blessing of the Ancestors, no Sklar-Quah boons).

Over-achievement: only 20 RP are required — a party that finishes the chapter with 30+ RP earns a bonus story award (full-encounter XP) for the outstanding showing.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, The Kallow Mounds — p.202.

Hundreds of skull-topped cairns (@Check[type:society|dc:15]: Skoan-Quah markers) — the three quahs' burial ground for 300 years, ~50 miles east of Kaer Maga (@Check[type:society|dc:25] to place it). ~75 Skull Clan camp here (12+ boneslayers always among them) under Chief One-Life, the shaman Ash Dancer — and Thousand Bones, who remembers Gaekhen. A boneslayer patrol intercepts at half a mile; his name (or Gaekhen's) opens the camp: a guest yurt, water, and a visit to Gaekhen's cairn (he approves if told Rolth was punished). Wicked-Claws, the camp's guardian, is a dragonne who lost his wings to a bulette; One-Life saved the proud beast, and it now suns itself atop a cairn by the chief's tent, grounded but watchful.

Krojun asks Chief One-Life, 'Why do the Skoan-Quah harbor tshamek trespassers?'

As Chief One-Life struggles to find an explanation that won't further enrage the towering visitor, Thousand Bones inclines his head as though considering Krojun's words carefully, but then responds sharply. 'Tell me, Krojun, when did the SklarQuah become judges of who trespasses upon the Kallow Mounds where the ashes of our fathers lie?'

'Your words change the question, Thousand Bones,' answers the Shoanti hero with a snort. 'These ones bring trouble to the Cinderlands, and you know it. The coming days shall reveal to us all who is right about them.'

'Perhaps,' says Thousand Bones. 'But not today, and not here. Would you have word that Berak's burial was tainted by bloodshed get back to your Sun Shaman?'

Krojun pauses, the cords in his neck straining, but then he exhales and grins. 'You misunderstand me, Thousand Bones. My grief has wounded my words. But see to it that no tshamek defiles our memories here.' His smile broadens as he pulls a thin leather loop from one of his packs. 'Certainly, though, guests of the Skoan-Quah must be brave to come this far. You wouldn't mind if I tested the courage and strength of your guests, would you?'

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun Eats-What-He-Kills} arrives bearing the hero Berak's body and bristles at outsiders; Thousand Bones defuses with the funeral. Then the challenge: sredna (@Check[type:society|dc:20] recognizes the leather loop; @Check[type:society|dc:30] or ask, for his story). Refusing costs −1 RP. He is secretly testing them: he wants a reason not to march his people into Ileosa's jaws.

SREDNA (converted): two contestants on hands and knees, bound head-to-head by a loop. Rounds 1–3: opposed Intimidation — winner +2 points, tie +1 each (pulling early disqualifies). Round 4+: initiative; each turn Tug (opposed Athletics; the defender who Dug In last round gets +4 circumstance; win +2 points, lose/tie +2 to the foe) or Dig In (+4 vs. the next tug). After each round past the 4th: each contestant attempts a Fortitude save, DC = the opponent's current points — fail and collapse (both fail = draw; Endurance-style abilities grant +4). Krojun rages if it passes six rounds.

RP: force his rage +1 · lose gamely +1 · draw +2 · win +3. Story award for earning 2+ RP from the game, even in defeat.

pages.name: The Bone Council Fire & Skoan-Quah Support

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, The Bone Council Fire — p.206.

At nightfall the camp withdraws in respect; only Thousand Bones, One-Life, Ash Dancer (sprinkling herbal dust on the flames — pleasant blur, no game effect), and the PCs' circle remain at the council fire.

'You have already done my people a great favor by returning the body of one of our warriors. I sense now you come to me to ask something in return, yet know that by asking for this, you are helping us all. The Skoan-Quah are a peaceful people, yet we are also all but shunned by our kin. Our willingness to mix with tshamek shames many of my brothers and sisters in the other quahs. Only their respect for our tradition of guarding and protecting the dead of all Shoanti keeps them from open hostility against us.

My words do not reach their ears when I warn them of Queen Ileosa and her rise in power. They hear tales of the city in flames, of its king dead, of disease ravaging its people, and they see this as a just punishment for a hated enemy. My people do not see that a greater threat is growing in this turmoil. And now, you come to me with concerns, seeking the aid of my people. Speak of what you wish of the Shoanti, and perhaps we may find our needs are the same.'

Hearing of Ileosa's invulnerability and her new crown confirms his fears. Asked about 'Midnight's Teeth' or the fangs, he answers:

'My people dwelt where your people live now, not so long ago. We remained there for many, many generations, but across the centuries my people have always kept the lore of our ancestors in mind, passing knowledge to the new generations. Yet when Cheliax came to us with war and drove us to the Cinderlands so many years ago, we fought. And died. And many of those who died took this lore to their graves.

The name 'Midnight's Teeth' is unfamiliar to me, but the name 'Kazavon' is not entirely so-it is a name associated with a great and ancient evil, and many Shoanti believe to repeat such a name aloud is to preserve the evil. This, coupled with the deaths of so many lore keepers, has sequestered the knowledge I suspect you seek in the minds of a rare few: the Sun Shamans of the Sklar-Quah. They alone preserve the history of the Shoanti time in the lands you now call Korvosa, but they do not readily share this with tshamek... or fellow Shoanti, for that matter. Yet if you were to build your names among my people, to earn proper respect, even the eldest of the Sun Shamans would agree to provide the knowledge you desire.'

The council names five paths to respect: become @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun's} nalharest · re-create Skurak's deed in @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cindermawWorm001]{Cindermaw's} belly (Handout #4-1) · win Truthspeaker @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.akramTruthspeak1]{Akram's} endorsement · earn the Thrallkeeper's Mark at the Acropolis · survive the Sklar-Quah Trial of the Totem. (@Check[type:arcana|dc:30] or @Check[type:society|dc:30]: the 'Thrallkeeper's Mark' is the Sihedron rune of old Thassilon.)

Thousand Bones's gifts: 5 healing potions (moderate), a wand of create water (44 charges), a wand of endure elements (23), 5 pots of @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appWarpaint00001]{Shoanti warpaint} (GM's pick of colors) — and four @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.skoanBoneslayer1]{boneslayer} escorts: Ahalak, Hargev, Nalmid, Shadfrar (guides, witnesses, and the party's most fragile asset — see their entry for the RP stakes). In the wastes, @Check[type:perception|dc:40] confirms the distant riders shadowing the party are Krojun's — he evades all confrontation until the Acropolis.

pages.name: Quest A: The Acropolis of the Thrallkeepers

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, A. The Acropolis of the Thrallkeepers — p.209.

The Acropolis — a Thassilonian conjuration complex whose masters died summoning the havero, 'Arms and Eyes of Forever,' from the Dark Tapestry; the part-summoned horror still hibernates below. Black granite flecked with blood-red crystal; every stone door bears a Sihedron bas-relief (press → it grinds up; descends after a minute; @Check[type:thievery|dc:15] jams one for a month; the closing crush deals @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] and pins — @Check[type:athletics|dc:25] to wrench free).

A crowded dungeon: 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassins} enter ~10 minutes behind the PCs (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderlander0001]{the Cinderlander} guided them but waits outside) — their real mission is to let @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu} watch the PCs fight through a scried assassin. And @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun} + 3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sklarThundercal1]{thundercallers} arrived an hour before, hiding at the observation gallery (A3) to take the party's measure (he mocks the Mark-quest as 'stargazing' — his laugh adds 2 Noise Points). @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:31] or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:23] brings the Shoanti in against the Mantis early (intimidated, they leave after and bad-mouth). The Shoanti automatically aid against the havero. Save them from certain death and Krojun may declare nalharest on the spot.

  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
The Mantis tailModerate · 80 XP @ L11

4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassins} — they strike once discovered (or as the PCs leave, if never engaged). Koriantu is watching; let the players feel it.

pages.name: A1-A3. Thrallkeeper's Walk, the Pool & the Gallery

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, A1-A3 — p.209.

The stone stairs end at a twenty-foot-wide and twentyfoot-tall hallway that leads to the east, opening into a large chamber. The floor is cluttered with dust and tiny mounds of ash that look to have been recently disturbed in places, but signs of the trail vanish to the east once the stones become clear of debris.

A1: the dust tells its story — @Check[type:survival|dc:4] notes four human-sized creatures recently passed from the stairs toward the east room; @Check[type:survival|dc:19] follows the trail to the A2/A3 door (where the Sklar-Quah wait). Within 10 feet of A2 the floor runs clean. If the assassins arrive while the PCs are busy in A2, two hide here while two Sneak inside; otherwise all four Stealth into A2 in pairs.

The air in this massive, cathedral-like space seems strangely cool. The walls are carved with vertical ridges that rise to support the arch above, where the ceiling vaults into the shadows to a height of nearly sixty feet. A five-foot-wide balcony rings the room, the floor of which drops fifteen feet into a pool of dark water. Halfway between the east and west ends of the room, a bridge crosses the pool. Two large stone doors stand in the walls to the south and east. Smaller doors sit in the walls to the northwest and southwest-all four doors bear depictions of seven-pointed-stars.

A2, Pool of the Havero: a 60-ft vault over a 15-ft pool of cold, algae-choked water floored with the sleeping horror's rubbery flank (closed bulbous eyes stud it) — the bottom is dimensionally adjacent to a thing orbiting a dead star. Narrow observation gaps in the north and south walls let watchers in the corridors beyond study this room (@Check[type:perception|dc:30] to spot the gaps from inside); @Check[type:survival|dc:21] tracks the Sklar-Quah across the floor toward A3.

NOISE POINTS (track openly): verbal spell +1 · running +1/runner · yell +1 · combat +5/round · sonic effect +10 · damaging the flank +1/point. Noise in A2 doubles. The total decays 1d10/minute. Thresholds (trigger the round after): 10 Twitch · 20 Investigate (1 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.haveroTentacle01]{tentacle}) · ~30 Seek · 40 Assault (2) · 50 Wrath (4, the max). Each slain tentacle −1d20 noise; below 10, all retract (quelled 1 hour). 20+ tentacles slain in one fight also quells it. Story award for crossing without waking it — or for quelling it once woken.

  • ≤4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.haveroTentacle01]{Havero Tentacle (9)}
Tentacle Wrath (worst case)Moderate · 80 XP @ L11

4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.haveroTentacle01]{havero tentacles} at 60-ft reach, attackable anywhere along their lengths. The Mantis watch; the Shoanti help.

One wall of this otherwise plain hallway features several long narrow gaps that open into the room beyond.

A3, Observation Point: the builders' 'safe' viewing gallery onto the summoning pool — and now @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun's} hide. Once the PCs leave A2, the Shoanti tail them as quietly as four armored barbarians can; when (likely) spotted, resolve via the Crowded Dungeon notes on the quest page.

pages.name: A4-A7. Shrines, the Illumacore & the Eternal Glyphs

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, A4-A7 — p.211.

The curved northern alcove of this chamber displays a brilliantly colored bas-relief carving of a woman with six wings and a serpentine lower body. The coils of her lower body wind in and out and around a large seven-pointed star. Tiny shards of gemstones embedded in her tail make her scales glisten as though with moisture, but in more places, chunks of the carving have cracked or crumbled away, giving the depiction of the serpentine woman a strange but undeniable look of age and decay.

A4: the serpent-woman shrine (@Check[type:religion|dc:25]: Kurshu the Undying, Lissala's herald, unprayed-to for millennia) — sapphire and emerald slivers in the tail, ~320 gp re-scaled (the thundercallers hoot at it: +2 Noise).

The floor of this otherwise empty chamber contains a fivefoot-diameter hole in the center, surrounded by a ring of tangled runes. Inside the hole, a shaft filled with brilliant emerald light drops into the depths.

A5, the Illumacore: a rune-ringed shaft of emerald light (@Check[type:arcana|dc:26] to deduce): step in → feather fall 70 ft down to A9; cross the lower ring → levitate back up (effects end on stepping out). A Mantis pair takes 2d4 rounds to puzzle it out before descending. Krojun goes first, to show off.

A strange bronze statue stands in the center of the room. The statue depicts a half-snake, half-human creature with the lower body of a serpent coiled about a stand and the upper torso of a slender human woman. Her hands are crossed over her chest to clutch two objects at her shoulders-the right hand holds a large quill, while the left holds a jade-handled whip. Six birdlike wings emerge from the torso's shoulders, and instead of a head, it has a disk bearing a seven-pointed star. Jade runes run down the statue's belly and along the length of its snaky lower body.

A6: the Lissala statue — her jade whip is a +2 axiomatic whip, cursed (1 Wisdom drain/day of visions; −6 Will saves while Thassilonian writing is visible; lifts only re-handed to the statue or remove curse vs. 18th). The base drawer (@Check[type:perception|dc:20] to notice) holds a 4-ft wedge-plate frame: align it (@Check[type:thievery|dc:20]) over the illumacore and the shaft-light projects the original acropolis map on the ceiling — today's rooms a small fraction of what sprawled here.

The walls of this chamber are decorated with six life-sized basrelief carvings that depict a diverse collection of priests, each adorned in billowing robes covered in Thassilonian runes. An intricate scripture winds around the carvings, coiling across them and along the walls like an immensely long tangle of ribbons. Several ancient skeletons, some clad in hide armor or clutching Shoanti weapons, lie scattered around the statue's base.

A7: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.eternalGlyphs001]{Eternal Glyphs} (@Check[type:religion|dc:30]: the figures are upper-tier Lissalan clergy) — and the gear of the Shoanti dead who starved reading (+1 hide, a +1 striking frost earth breaker, a +1 striking returning totem spear). Entranced PCs are easy Red Mantis prey; if Krojun must drag the party out: −1d6 RP.

pages.name: A8-A11. The Lower Level & the Golarion Globe

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, A8-A11 — p.213.

This corridor ends abruptly, choked off by thousands of tons of collapsed rock and sand.

A8, Collapsed Corridors: wrecked in the havero's first thrashing and finished by centuries of erosion — impassable. North once led to the Thrallkeepers' opulent quarters and their lesser summoning chambers; south, to the slave pens and thrall common rooms.

This wide corridor, composed of polished black granite, runs north to south. A blue stone disc sits on the floor to the south, with a glowing column of blue light rising up from the disc through a hole in the ceiling. To the north, a five-foot-wide circular opening in the wall opens into a smaller chamber. The ceiling here is only fifteen feet high.

A9: beyond the havero's reach — noise down here cannot rouse it unless truly thunderous (the Mantis love an ambush ringing the illumacore landing, or set north to catch PCs leaving A11). A 120-ton stone plug (@Check[type:perception|dc:22]) hides the hallway east.

A jumble of five crushed humanoid skeletons lie in the southeast corner of this chamber. Stone niches in the southern wall contain a few pieces of clutter.

A10, the Fallback Vault (the plug: Hardness 8 and absurd HP; a gaseous-form gap remains — the Thrallkeepers themselves teleported in): the five dead Thrallkeepers' gear — a bottle of air, decanter of endless water, sustaining spoon (abnormally spicy gruel), scrolls (2× clairvoyance, 2× sending, 2× dimension door), a +1 striking magical-beast-bane dagger, a bright-orange cloak of resistance +3 (greater resilient rune), a scabbard of keen edges, and ~700 gp re-scaled in jewelry, tableware, and a Lissalan holy symbol. The bones and cloth crumble at a touch.

The walls, ceiling, and floor of this chamber have been painted to resemble a starry sky, giving the illusion of walking through space. A ten-foot-diameter stone sphere floats five feet above the floor near the room's southern wall. Three short metal rods protrude from the lower hemisphere, radiating outward like a tripod, only the rods rest on empty air rather than a solid surface. The sphere appears to have been carefully carved with tiny rivers, mountains, oceans, and forests.

A11, the Golarion Globe: a hollow 10-ft stone globe of the pre-Earthfall world (@Check[type:society|dc:20] — landlocked Varisia, no Inner Sea, continents that no longer exist; @Check[type:arcana|dc:30] on its aura deduces its function and the exit method) floating on three immovable rods. Touch it and contemplate the stars → drawn inside into suspended animation and space-visions (@Check[type:will|dc:15] per minute or 1d4 Intelligence drain; concentrate on Golarion to exit; auto-ejected at Int 0). Exit grants the THRALLKEEPER'S MARK (one palm, one week, once per year): right palm = banishment 1/day; left = summon (5th rank) 1/day; either grants +4 status vs. mental. Pulling a rod drops the globe (+10 Noise; @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] to anyone beneath, @Check[type:reflex|dc:15] avoids; it cracks — 1d4 more weeks of function). +3 RP for gaining the Mark before Shoanti eyes; story award if any PC gains it.

A Parting of Ways: if no decisive Red Mantis clash has happened, they strike as the PCs leave the ruins. The Sklar-Quah depart once the Mark is won — and at your discretion Krojun offers to guide the party to Flameford if that is their next road.

pages.name: Quest B: The House of the Moon

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, B. House of the Moon — p.218.

A silvery stone shrine stands atop a low promontory in the foothills here. The surrounding stone has been smoothed by ages of wind, but the shrine itself remains as stark and unweathered as the day its final block was set in place. The structure shimmers with a slightly reflective sheen, and even on moonless nights it glimmers with lunar radiance. A 30-foot-tall opening allows access to the shrine interior at ground level.

The curving sides of the archway are framed by the long peacock-like tail feathers of an immense star monarch carved into the building's facade. The creature's wings furl to the left and right, fanning over two side towers attached to the central spire, which rises to a height of 100 feet. Above, a silvery sphere caps the central tower's peak, as if the moon had fallen out of the sky to become impaled upon the steeple.

Thousand Bones's pointer: Truthspeaker Akram arrives with the Lyrune-Quah in days — but a red reaver has taken the shrine, eating a dozen moon maidens and their commander. Tekrakai's surviving band of four (@Check[type:perception|dc:20] on Sense Motive reads their worry; @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:30] or @Check[type:intimidation|dc:23] to bench them — but doing so costs −2d4 RP) wants it dead; her briefing is exact: rending claws, a discord-roar, distracted by artistic beauty, never sleeps. Killing it unannounced earns the same gratitude.

B1, the gathering hall: a 20-ft white-stone starknife on a pivot — a cunningly balanced proto-calendar that priests rotate by hand to mark month and day; any Shoanti reads at a glance that no one has worshiped here in months. Picked-over carcasses and the seven dead maidens are heaped in the southwest corner; the reaver lairs here. B2, the sky well: a moonlike sphere whose curtain of light paints the ceiling with the true night sky at any hour, in any weather — an all-weather observatory.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redReaver0000001]{Red Reaver (13)}
  • 4× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.moonMaiden000001]{Moon Maiden (6, allies)}
The Red ReaverModerate · 80 XP @ L11

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redReaver0000001]{the Red Reaver} (at ~85% HP from old wounds) in the moonlit hall — walls of self-repairing blessed masonry (Hardness 16; SR vs. manipulation). It targets the heaviest armor, pursues half a mile, fights to the death. A performance (@Check[type:performance|dc:31]) buys calm rounds. Story award per surviving maiden.

Among the seven dead maidens: 3× +1 hide armor and 5× +1 starknives (the Lyrune-Quah gift them to helpers). The shrine itself: Desnan sleepers receive a yearly dream-commune from the avoral Nightspear; the sky-well projects the true night sky at all hours.

After: the tribe arrives; @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.akramTruthspeak1]{Akram} agrees to travel as witness (+3 RP, story award; second award if he lives to the Sun Shaman's tale). He is 68 and frail; honor forbids him an escort. Keep. Him. Alive.

pages.name: Quest C: The Belly of the Beast (Cindermaw)

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, C. Belly of the Beast — p.224.

Skurak's Tale (Handout #4-1):

Skurak was a great warrior and greater traitor to the Sklar-Quah. He slew his brother, a man of even greater courage. To the Sklar-Quah, family is purity-crimes against family are the greatest one can commit. Although Skurak claimed the death was an accident that occurred while he and his brother were hunting, others spoke of murder spawned of jealous rage. Skurak was declared a tshamek by the Sun Shaman and cast out. But before Skurak left, he said he would be born again and return to his tribe.

This he did. He went to the killing grounds of great Cindermaw the Clan-Eater. Skurak walked up to the beast carrying only his dagger. Without fear he dove into the beast's mouth and cut his way out. He returned to the clan and declared he had been reborn, and had left his misdeeds behind in the cleansing fire of Cindermaw's belly. The legend says the Sun Shaman accepted this and Skurak's time as a tshamek was spoken of no more.

The deed: one PC swallowed by @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cindermawWorm001]{Cindermaw} and out again, before Shoanti witnesses. The method is irrelevant — cut free (Rupture 30; fire resistance advised), teleport from the belly (concentration inside), even charm. Tempting a swallow: Deception at −2 vs. its Sense Motive. An illusion of the deed must beat the witnesses' saves and radiate heat. Hunt it near the western cindercones; it finds loiterers — stage its spark-gout surfacings first.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cindermawWorm001]{Cindermaw (14)}
Cindermaw (do not slay)Severe · 120 XP @ L11

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cindermawWorm001]{Cindermaw} — Severe+ if fought honestly, which is the wrong lesson: killing it costs RP (a dead god 'wasn't that tough'). +2 RP for the deed; +4 if the worm lives.

pages.name: Quest D: Flameford & the Trial of the Totem

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, D. Flameford — p.226.

Flameford — 52 Sklar-Quah on a cliff-edge behind permanent spike stones fields (@Check[type:perception|dc:29] per square; hidden zigzag paths), under Chief Ready-Klar (Krojun's uncle) and the nameless Sun Shaman. A six-rider burn-rider patrol laughs at the PCs' goal but escorts them in, armed.

Points of interest: D1 The Approach — the spike-stone fields and their unmarked safe paths (above); D2 War Council Fire — the camp's nightly feasting-and-debate heart; D3 Corral — eight horses, one per burn rider; D4 Sun Shaman's Yurt — the nameless shaman's home (the title is taken with the name's surrender); D5 Chieftain's Yurt — the largest, three-quarters communal feasting hall, the northeast quarter Ready-Klar's own; D6 Guest Yurt — the PCs' lodging: furs, one small fire pit, nothing else. Mostly L1–3 warriors dwell here, plus 6 thundercallers and 8 burn riders.

At the Foot of the Fire: the whole camp gathers; @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:29] mollifies the chieftain (+10 circumstance with @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.akramTruthspeak1]{Akram}; @Check[type:intimidation|dc:24] works instead) — +2 RP. At 20 RP, skip to the Assault; under it, the Sun Shaman grants the Trial of the Totem (Krojun storms off on a long ride). Mentioning Kazavon makes the old man pale and refuse. Akram departs before the Sklar-Quah dawn, advising: win Krojun.

THE TRIAL: haul fired-clay sun totems (10 ft, 500 lbs; Hardness 6, HP 40) up Bolt Rock — to the 30-ft tier by day one, the 40-ft mesa by day two, each kept standing through its day; the third dawn crowns success. Teamwork legal; no food or water provided (bring your own; endure elements and create food are fair); altering totem or rock is cheating — the Sun Shaman inspects (@Check[type:survival|dc:20] per alteration to hide; 3+ immovable rods leave no trace). Steadying: @Check[type:athletics|dc:12] every 6 hours; a failure leans the totem (@Check[type:athletics|dc:16] to catch); a second topples it (@Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] to the totem — shattered = that PC is out). Day two without sleep: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued}; run heat and thirst by the environment rules.

The Sun Vision: each hour fatigued, 10% cumulative chance (+30%/hour exhausted) of the aurochs-vision — two easy Wisdom-flavored insights: she is the aurochs… and she is not prey. A PC who completes it gains +1 insight to attack and damage vs. the Red Mantis and their minions for the chapter. The message: stop waiting. Turn and face them.

  • 6× @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.CzxQpB3p0d9hwPeR]{Bulette (8)}
Hungry Visitors (day 2, exhausted)Moderate · 90 XP @ L11

6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.CzxQpB3p0d9hwPeR]{bulettes} leap the lower tier (2d4 rounds of burrowing warning). Fighting one-handed on a totem: -2 to attacks; a bulette passing a totem forces @Check[type:athletics|dc:15] or it topples; misses by 3 or less against a holder strike the totem half the time. Dropping a totem on a bulette: @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]] (@Check[type:reflex|dc:15] avoids) — and the totem takes the same.

The Third Dawn: the tribe ascends pre-dawn; one standing totem makes the whole party Sklar-Quah-accepted (its keeper a citizen; the rest honored nalharests). The Sun Shaman casts lesser restoration on each PC. +1 RP for the attempt, +1 per totem that never toppled; story award if at least one stood. Krojun, returned and calmed, watches day two from the rocks and grudgingly calls the PCs lucky in their nalharests.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, The Flameford Assault / Saving Krojun — p.230.

The Assault triggers at 20 RP: the Sun Shaman must first commune at the Kallow Mounds (wind walk, back in a day; Krojun commands). At dusk the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ashwingGargoyl01]{Ashwings} lift the Red Mantis over the spike-fields and drop them among the yurts.

  • 3-6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
  • 3-6× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ashwingGargoyl01]{Ashwing Gargoyle (6)}
The AssaultLow · 60 XP @ L11

If the vault still stands / PCs L11: 3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassins} + 3 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ashwingGargoyl01]{Ashwing gargoyles}. If the hideout fell first / PCs L12: double it (6+6 — Moderate · 90 XP @ L12), adding @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} if she escaped with a grudge. The lead assassin names the PCs and offers the Shoanti peace for their heads: with 5+ RP the Shoanti refuse; with 4 or fewer they stand aside and the PCs face the full force (6 assassins, the Cinderlander, two dozen gargoyles). The tribal battle runs offstage: if the PCs win their fight, most Shoanti live. +4 RP for breaking the assault.

Saving Krojun: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderlander0001]{Cinderlander's} price for guiding the Mantis was Krojun. As the PCs finish their fight, the duel still rages — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun} at ~30 HP against the hunter's ~100 (Low · 60 XP @ L11). Unaided, Krojun dies and the killer walks. Intervene: the Cinderlander flees below ~20 HP; Krojun, saved, declares the PCs nalharest — +3 RP and a story award equal to his own defeat. The aftermath is a victory feast: sredna rematches, Sklar-Quah tattoos, a marriage offer contingent on shaving a PC's head.

If the PCs fall, the Shoanti are wiped out — the merciful out: survivors wake stripped and imprisoned in the Deathhead Vault, which conveniently is where Part 3 goes anyway.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, Part 3: Mantis and Maiden — p.234.

Part 3 — Korvosa, meanwhile: the Sable Company disbanded, the Hellknights withdrawn, the Guard gutted; Abadar's archbanker and the Asmodeans back the queen (seeding schism among young clergy); only Pharasma's church quietly resists; the Red Mantis kill notables in daylight; Ileosa's endgame — thousands sacrificed for eternal youth — gathers. Do not start before L12 (the mission keeps: it can wait for an overland return, or even until early Ch.5 forays). If the PCs aren't in the city, Keppira d'Bear's sending reaches them:

'Return to Korvosa! An opportunity to strike at our enemies has presented itself. The Mantis and Maidens will fall! Seek us in the Dead Warrens!'

An Unexpected Opportunity: the rebel HQ is the old Dead Warrens ossuary (Ch.1!), warded (private sanctum + forbiddance) and password-keyed ('Blackjack', from Kroft at the door). Inside wait Keppira d'Bear — and a florid, heavyset stranger Kroft bitterly tolerates: Guildmaster Boule of the Cerulean Society (@Check[type:society|dc:20] to place him). 'Tell them, Boule. Tell them why I'm tolerating your presence.'

'Yes, let me get right to the point. We may operate on different sides of the law, but we can agree that Ileosa is not good for Korvosa. I want her gone as much as you. And while I feel that neither I nor my... associates... are the right ones for the task, you, brave adventurers, have proven time and time again that you are precisely what Korvosa needs.

'Without the Gray Maidens to police the streets or the Red Mantis to stalk the alleys, Ileosa's grip on Korvosa will slip. Yet in their fortress at the Longacre Building, the Red Mantis and the Gray Maidens are bolstered against attack, and many who work within the building may yet be innocent, forced to comply with the queen against their will. But as it happens, I know of a back door into the chambers below. I know the secret to Deathhead Vault.'

The secret (@Check[type:society|dc:10] knows the vault: where Korvosa's most violent criminals are jailed and often executed): for years the Society traded 'stock' to the derros in the caverns adjoining the vault; in exchange the guild smuggled prisoners out through the derro warrens, swapping in insane patsies. The derros have been silent for months — Boule is convinced the Gray Maidens and Red Mantis found the caverns and use them now. The sewer secret door still works. His extras: a partial key, directions (+20 circumstance to the E1/E2, E6, and E9 secret doors) — and fake 'Korvosan Revolution' ciphers leaked to @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kordaitraDesta01]{Kordaitra}, nailing the would-be codebreaker to her quarters for at least a week. The Arkona papers (D16): shown he's compromised, Boule pales and offers 600 pp per PC (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:25] raises it to 900; story award for brokering favors for the Korvosan Guard instead of coin, Kroft's preference — and if the PCs keep copies and Boule learns of it, he retaliates).

Objectives (Kroft): rescue Marcus Endrin · recover the records · learn why Arbiter Zenobia sided with the queen · break the Red Mantis and Gray Maiden leadership. Doors: stone in the west (E1-E9: Hardness 8, HP 60), locked reinforced wood in the Vault proper (E10-E21: @Check[type:thievery|dc:40] or carried keys).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, E1-E4 — p.237.

A ten-foot-wide trough of murky water fills two-thirds of this wide, brick-lined tunnel. A slimy five-foot-wide walkway runs along the east side of the waterway. A wall of rusty iron bars with an equally rusty gate atop the walkway blocks passage further into the tunnel. Further to the south, the tunnel deadends at an ancient collapse.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.RA2dkQJANNFjgdSt]{Catoblepas (12)}
E1 — SnagglebreathTrivial · 40 XP @ L12

An albino @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.RA2dkQJANNFjgdSt]{catoblepas} — Cinnabar's imported pet (@Check[type:perception|dc:15] spots the painted rust; its mithral-spiked collar, engraved 'Snagglebreath,' ~100 gp re-scaled). It loves Red Mantis colors and savages everyone else. The secret door beyond: @Check[type:perception|dc:40] (+20 with Boule's directions).

The air in this room smells strongly of frankincense and is hazy with smoke from several incense burners mounted on the walls, yet a slight whiff of the stench from the sewers to the west nonetheless manages to linger faintly in the background. A pair of rickety chairs with moldy cushions sit to the south, while to the north stands a strange incongruity-a wooden bar, complete with shelves filled with bottles and tankards on the walls. A wooden door is set into the northeast corner of the room, amid all the shelves behind the bar.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.DlRe4c2XlBSpwmms]{Mogmora (8, Elite)}
E2 — the 'Sewer Brewer'Trivial · 10 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.DlRe4c2XlBSpwmms]{Mogmora} (an Elite greater barghest in goblin form — effectively L8), planar-bound bartender of the Society's old smuggling waiting-room dressed as a tavern, offering his 'lucky first customers' free ale poisoned with lich dust (party-size + 4 doses behind the bar; the display bottles are tinted sewer water). He stalls 1d4 rounds with backhanded compliments, cataloguing numbers, arms, and faces — then feigns terror and dimension doors to E6 to report to Koriantu. His key (or Boule's) opens the north door.

The tunnel opens into a wide cavern with a high vaulted ceiling that arches up to a height of thirty feet. Two ten-foot-high ledges look out over the wall from the northwest wall, while to the east the cavern dips down into a pool of dark water. Swaths of softly glowing blue fungus fill the cave with light; the fungus grows in lumps that bear an eerie resemblance to brains. To the southeast, a fifteen-foot-wide tunnel is bisected by a deep pit. A pair of three-foot-wide planks forms a treacherous-looking bridge over the pit.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.dispellingMist01]{Dispelling Mist (8)}
  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.manananggal00001]{Manananggal (9)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
E3 — the ChokepointTrivial · 45 XP @ L12

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.dispellingMist01]{Dispelling Mist} at the entry; a 60-ft spike-rocked pit (@Damage[8d6[bludgeoning]]) bridged by a permanent-image plank (@Check[type:will|dc:17] on interaction — the assassins jump or climb, @Check[type:athletics|dc:15] on the walls); the east pool is drinkable but bitter (the garrison's water source); cytillesh patches (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied} creep within 20 ft per day — the assassins ration exposure under 20 hours); and 2 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.manananggal00001]{manananggals} — the late Sable officers Banzul and Wevenner — plus a hidden @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis assassin} beyond the pit who watches two rounds, then slips to E4 to report.

By night the pair slumber-bomb from the ledges and swoop; by day they wear their human faces and cry out as 'escaped prisoners of the derros,' begging rescue from atop the ledges — bait to split the party and draw one or two climbers into reach (they keep bite and claw in human guise). Their torsos lie in E8: destroy those to end them at dawn.

The arched ceiling of this vast, crescent-shaped, cathedrallike cavern rises to a height of nearly forty feet, supported by four natural stone columns. These columns are caked with glowing blue swaths of brain-shaped fungi, while a larger section of the stuff grows along the curved wall to the north and east. A total of nine dark openings near the ceiling to the south and west can be reached only by climbing or flight. To the southeast, eight stone benches face a single pulpit that stands before a flight of stairs. The stairs lead up to a worked stone facade that bears a looming carving of an immense mantis, its arms arrayed downward like an archway over a set of double doors.

  • 2× @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{Red Mantis Assassin (9)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cytillipede00001]{Cytillipede (8)}
E4 — the Temple of AchaekekTrivial · 40 XP @ L12

The mantis façade is fresh stone shape (@Check[type:arcana|dc:28]); @Check[type:religion|dc:15] names Achaekek. Most of Korvosa's assassins rotate through here a few nights a week (cytillesh caps each stay under 20 hours) — but with the bulk out hunting the PCs, only 2 resting @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redMantisAssn001]{assassins} + the mercy-bought @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cytillipede00001]{cytillipede} (flash timed for maximum coverage, sneak attacks on the stunned) are present. If the alarm is up they hide among the benches, everything that fled converges here, and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu} stand behind them (Extreme · 175 XP @ L12 — the vault's worst case).

E4a: the north tunnel once wound down to deeper derro warrens and, beyond, to Nar-Voth in the Darklands — the Gray Maidens collapsed it after the derro purge. What lies past the rubble is beyond this chapter; if the PCs dig, stock it with Darklands material of your own.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, E5-E6 — p.242.

Three large stone vats sit in alcoves in this room. Each is big enough to hold a human, but apart from a strange blue residue caking the insides of each, they are empty. A cot and a writing desk sit amid the vats-awkward additions of comfort in what once seems to have been a strange laboratory.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar (13)}
E5 — CinnabarLow · 60 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} in her spartan chambers — masked, mantis-formed, summoning. Read her morale before running this: the kneeling surrender at ~10 HP is a genuine fork in the campaign. The desk holds training notes, Achaekek prayers, and clinical anatomy sketches — nothing tying the Mantis to the Crown — but @Check[type:perception|dc:25] finds a crimson parchment wedged between two drawers under secret page (a faint aura): speak 'Kayltanya' to reveal Handout #4-2.

Handout #4-2 — the Mistress's letter (read aloud once the password is spoken):

Cinnabar —

Agent Koriantu's reports of your progress in Korvosa paints a very different picture than that you've presented previously to the Crimson Citadel, and we are displeased you have downplayed the facts. In particular, we find the disruptions inflicted on the operation by the adventurers you claimed would be 'simple matters to resolve' to be particularly worrisome.

If the agents under your command can defeat these adventurers in the Cinderlands as you promise, well and good, but should they continue to act as flies in the ointment, I shall be obliged to relieve you of duty and lead the operation myself. The Blood Mistress is already unhappy with the, shall we say, sloppiness your agents have exhibited to date, and if I am forced to step in to take command of a continually faltering operation, one would hope for your sake that you have not survived this latest mishandling of resources.

Any pains inflicted upon you by your simple little adventurers would feel as a lover's tender caress compared to my ministrations. Trusting you will do what is needed, and may you walk in blood.

— K.

The letter is the chapter's one hard proof binding the Red Mantis contract to the Crown — and the first naming of Mistress Kayltanya, who makes good on it in Ch.6.

Six stone pillars support the roof of this T-shaped chamber. The walls are decorated with incredibly detailed paintings of immense mantis-like monsters destroying towns with various forms of architecture. Strangely, the scenes constantly evolve, playing out an eternal scene of slaughter. The most impressive feature in the chamber is a towering stone statue of a fourarmed mantis to the west.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu (13)}
E6 — the Inner SanctumLow · 60 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.koriantuPriest01]{Koriantu} behind her screen-illusion of slaughter-murals and a towering mantis idol (@Check[type:will|dc:32] on interaction; only the pillars are real — beneath it, a plain meeting room where derros and Society thieves once traded prisoners). The 'statue' hides her cot, altar, and scrying bowl; the illusion also hides the north door (findable by touch). Blade barrier, void, and zero personal stake in the PCs — she came to document Cinnabar's failure (and kills Cinnabar herself if the kind self surfaces). At ~40 HP she word of recalls to the Crimson Citadel — re-encounter her beside Mistress Kayltanya in Ch.6. South secret door: @Check[type:perception|dc:40] (+20 from Boule; −20 while fooled by the screen).

pages.name: E7-E11. Crypts, the Daemon & the Mother of Thorns

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, E7-E11 — p.245.

Alcoves along the east and west walls of this hallway contain ancient stone sarcophagi.

E7: looted Shoanti sarcophagi (@Check[type:society|dc:25]), used since as derro cages. Nothing of value remains.

The walls of this large room bear ancient carvings of wild animals in a scene set within a rugged, barren landscape. Two sarcophagi, their lids pushed aside, sit in alcoves to the north and east, while to the west is a third sarcophagus upon a raised dais surrounded by several three-foot-tall vats. The rank smell of vinegar fills the air.

E8, Zenobia's Crypt: once a great Shoanti shaman's tomb (@Check[type:society|dc:25] reads the carvings), then a derro necromantic workshop. By night her headless body lies in the west sarcophagus and the manananggals' torsos in the others (@Check[type:religion|dc:27]: destroying torsos destroys their owners; destroying Zenobia's body only ends her living masquerade — the vinegar vats are how she fits back into it).

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vylothMeladaem01]{Vyloth (13)}
E8 — VylothLow · 60 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vylothMeladaem01]{Vyloth}, the bored famine-daemon, opens with his lying question-game — then Horrid Wilting. If the body is destroyed he teleports to Zenobia (E18) and is fought there instead.

E9: the Society's secret stair (@Check[type:perception|dc:40] at either end, +20 from Boule) — saboteur work so audacious the arbiters never thought to look for it.

The walls of this ten-foot-wide hallway have been set with polished ivory tiles, each of which bears a softly glowing circle of light.

E10, the Final Walk: the condemned's corridor from cell block to execution chamber; the calm emotions effect once woven into its walls is long disarmed — only the dim glow remains.

Softly glowing ivory tiles adorn the walls, floor, and ceiling of this room. Four wooden benches sit in the center of the room, facing an upraised area on which an executioner's block sits. To the north, an intimidating-looking greataxe hangs on display on a sturdy weapon rack. An iron door in the west wall, just north of the main double-door entrance, bears a narrow window, currently closed via a sliding metal slat.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.motherOfThorns01]{Mother of Thorns (13)}
E11 — the Mother of ThornsLow · 60 XP @ L12

Korvosa's old beheading chamber (the worst of the condemned died here within three minutes of delivery, priests of Abadar and Pharasma and three arbiters attending) — now the scar-carving room, where every recruit's ritual facial wounds are cut. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.motherOfThorns01]{The Mother of Thorns} attacks on sight — vines at 15 feet, fear to scatter, domination for the strongest — narrating to Kordaitra telepathically all the while. At ~40 HP she teleports to E18, heals with her wand at Zenobia's side, and waits for the rematch; fighting beside Zenobia she fights to the death.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, E12-E21 — p.248.

Two writing desks are pushed up against the west wall of this room, opposite a freestanding wooden cabinet and an iron door with a window set in it.

E12 — Observation office: the old execution-records office (the files still moulder in the cabinet); the commanders watch the Mother of Thorns work through the window, coming and going by the secret tunnel (@Check[type:perception|dc:30] for its doors).

E13 — Deathhead Cells: cot-and-chamber-pot cells holding 12 imprisoned @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch4GMRecruit0001]{recruits} who refused the Maidens — beaten to 1d4 HP, no condition to aid anyone (Boule's or Kordaitra's keys, or @Check[type:thievery|dc:40] on the iron gates) — story award per woman freed (15 total with E18). E14 — The secret cell: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.marcusEndrin0001]{Marcus Endrin} (see his entry; Boule's advice does not help find this door — the E20 records reveal it, +20 circumstance).

The western wall of this tiled hallway is a row of bars that cages a smaller room within, accessible by a heavy iron gate. Within the smaller room sits a desk, a filing cabinet, and a table. A window in the bars allows for a place to perhaps pass objects from the hall to the cage.

E15 — Indoctrination chamber: the old prisoner-processing room, now processing recruits. 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenGuard1]{Gray Maiden Guards} — two caged (firing through the bars with cover; one shouts the alarm before fighting) and two in the hall. The locked northwest door climbs to the Longacre Building's ground floor, where a few dozen lower-ranking Maidens are quartered — but they are forbidden the vault: avoid the upper floors and no reinforcements ever come down (the floors above are beyond this chapter's scope).

Each of these identical rooms features a single bed and an armor and weapon stand.

E16 — Guard quarters: 4 resting @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenGuard1]{guards}; on an alarm they pile into one room to buckle each other in — ~5 minutes before they can join a fight in E15.

This large room has been outfitted to serve as both a study and a bedroom. Several important-looking documents sit on a table to the south, while an elegant suit of silvery, feminine armor is on display to the north.

E17 — Tisharue: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.tisharueWarden01]{the warden}, her mother's displayed +2 resilient mithral chain (Tanglebriar heirloom), and her dossier — conditioning and scar records with Elven margin notes concluding Sabina Merrin was barely conditioned: leverage she hoards against the commander.

This austere bedroom doesn't appear to have been used in some time.

E19 — Vavana's room: empty since Vavana Dhatri's reassignment to Castle Korvosa — a Ch.6 thread.

Dozens of scrolls lie on the shelves adorning the walls of this room.

E20 — The records: every woman recruited and conditioned, every failure 'executed as a traitor' (in truth few survive the torments that long), no Crown–Mantis paperwork — but closure for the families of the lost, and the location of Endrin's secret cell (E14). Story award for recovering them.

This grim chamber is outfitted with all manner of torture implements: a rack, several cages, a cramped stockade with spiky bars, and smaller tools of torture like thumbscrews and iron boots sitting on shelves. A caged-off area to the west serves as a guard post.

E18 — the Maiden's Nursery (climax)Extreme · 170 XP @ L12

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zenobiaZenderh01]{Zenobia} mid-indoctrination of one of 3 caged @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch4GMRecruit0001]{recruits} behind a 5-@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grayMaidenGuard1]{guard} watch post (locked gate) — alone, a mild fight for L12 PCs (Trivial · 30 XP @ L12). The label above is the alarm-stacked worst case: prisoners caged, guards out front, @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kordaitraDesta01]{Kordaitra} relocating from E21 with her Shield Wall plan pre-briefed (round 1 her order gives Zenobia a double-cast; divine power comes back round 3) — plus @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.motherOfThorns01]{the Mother of Thorns} and/or @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vylothMeladaem01]{Vyloth} fallen back per their morale. Thin the pile before it stacks. Zenobia flees at ~40 HP (her sending warns Vavana; re-encounter her in Castle Korvosa); Kordaitra below ~40 HP bolts for her hidden room and escapes to the castle on dust of disappearance + gaseous form.

This bedroom's decor reveals little about its occupant, other than that it must be a clean, well-ordered, militaristic person with little interest in decor. The monochromatic furniture is blocky and functional, and only the large landscape painting of Korvosa, presented in lovely colors, brings any personality to the chamber at all.

E21 — Kordaitra: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kordaitraDesta01]{the conditioner} at Boule's ciphers (≥1 week to crack; if she succeeds first she decamps to the castle with everything below). The painting — Sinister Sister, Goren Andosalu of Magnimar, 3×6 ft, 40 lbs — ~450 gp re-scaled. Secret door @Check[type:perception|dc:30] (locked, @Check[type:thievery|dc:40] or her key) to the emergency repository: a cherrywood coffer (~50 gp) of 4 moderate healing potions (cure serious), 3 potions of remove disease, 6 potions of lesser restoration, and 3 potions of gaseous form; plus 2 doses of dust of disappearance, a wand of cure critical wounds (40 charges), scrolls of break enchantment, heal, limited wish, and resurrection, 2 candles of truth, and 3 doses of stone salve — all carried in a bag of holding (type IV). Her key ring also holds the treasury key (see her entry — it matters in Ch.6).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.4, Chapter Conclusion — p.254.

Bolstering the Rebellion: the vault broken, the Red Mantis quit Korvosa (returning only with Mistress Kayltanya in Ch.6), the Maidens fall back to the castle, martial law ends — run Korvosa as Unrest hereafter, and let the Epidemic Clock ease accordingly.

The Sun Shaman's Tale (at 20 RP, after he communes at the Kallow Mounds): he summons the PCs to his yurt, congratulates them on shedding tshamek status, and speaks by his fire:

'Many hundreds of years ago, a man named Mandraivus gathered a small group of heroes to fight a despotic blue dragon named Kazavon, and my ancestor was one who joined this crusade. This ancestor, a shaman named Amarund, was gone for months. When she finally returned she was not the same woman-her hands shook, her eyes carried a haunted stare, and she cried out in fear at night. She spoke little of what she had experienced while fighting at Mandraivus's side, but did say that they were successful in defeating Kazavon and that the dragon's fortress of Scarwall in Belkzen was now under Mandraivus's control.

'While Kazavon had been defeated, his will to live was so immense that even the remains of his body twitched. The heroes attempted to destroy the remains, but key fragments of the dragon's skeleton resisted even their most destructive spells. Mandraivus tasked the seven surviving heroes, among them Amarund, with each claiming one of the bony relics of Kazavon's body and taking them far from Scarwall. None of the seven would communicate where they were going to the others or to Mandraivus in hopes of ensuring that their chosen relics would remain hidden and guarded for all time in order to prevent the dragon from returning to life.

'Amarund told this story to her fellow shamans and revealed that her selected relics were the dragon's fangs, which she called Midnight's Teeth. She and the other shamans chose the ancient pyramid on the shores of Conqueror's Bay as the fangs' reliquary. After hiding them in a secret room deep inside the pyramid, Amarund and her descendants swore to ensure the fangs would remain safe. For generations, they maintained their task-until Cheliax invaded and drove us off. The few survivors who knew the secret of the fangs were forced to flee with their kin into the Cinderlands. For the next three centuries the knowledge was passed from Sun Shaman to Sun Shaman, and we have watched with fearful eyes as the city of Korvosa has grown up around our ancient reliquary.'

What it means: the soul-fragments are seeds — rooted in Ileosa, Kazavon's has bloomed; she carries two souls, and while the second ripens she commands her own mortality (he nods grimly at news of the failed assassination). The counterstroke sleeps at Scarwall in Belkzen, where Serithtial waits among the chained dead. Chapter 5's road.

The Blessing of the Ancestors (upper tier of Bolt Rock; 2 hours' preparation, begun 2 hours before sunrise so it ends at dawn; the tribe attends unless asked not to; participants ring the Sun Shaman and his fire, each touching the next — 10 minutes of sung legends, then two hours of wordless droning, then the spirit rises from the smoke). The strongest conduit is Zellara — if the players miss it, her deck-bearer feels the overwhelming empathic wave (a shaman/spiritualist PC's companion also serves). The spirit greets the PCs by name and offers the fifth Harrowing; asked how the enemy can be defeated, it sings:

Handout #4-3 — The Spirit's Song:

Fate of steel-Serithtial Her cage for years sustained.
Four enthralled in lost Scarwall;
Undead to keep her chained.
A spirit first, red war his thirst Still stands at post of old;
A second foe, infernal soul Waits high in tower cold.
In kennel's grime, third bides his time Then vents his killing breath.
And on a stone 'mid ash and bone, The final dreams of death.
The spirits worn and battle torn And locked in their damnation, The chained one's hold at last grows old And ushers in salvation.
Yet hope remains amid the chains When blade's stone cage has crumbled, Friends to dread and death of the dead, Keys to Kazavon humbled.

GM note — this is the Ch.5 puzzle key. The four verses name Scarwall's four chained spirit-anchors, one per stanza: the war-spirit at his old post, the infernal soul high in a cold tower, the kennel-dweller with the killing breath, and the dreamer on a stone amid ash and bone. 'Humbling' all four is what finally unbinds Serithtial. Keep the handout where the players can re-read it all of next chapter.

Boons: each PC infuses one owned weapon (undead-bane) or armor/shield (ghost touch) — functioning only within Castle Scarwall — and gains Infused Soul: the next failed save vs. a death effect is negated instead (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} for a round; once, works anywhere). Ready-Klar proclaims the PCs blessed Friends of the Sun, who go forth with Father Sun's power in their hands.
